news.title: Prime Securities group company launches ₹1,000 crore real estate AIF
news.para: Prime Litmus Real Estate Opportunities Fund, a Category II AIF, has a target corpus of ₹750 crore and a green shoe option of ₹250 crore. The fund will invest in structured credit opportunities in under-construction real estate projects across major markets.

news.title: Shreyas Iyer leases residential property in Worli, Mumbai for ₹7.14 crore over three years
news.para: Cricketer Shreyas Iyer has secured a luxurious Mumbai residence in Worli's Artesia, agreeing to a substantial monthly rent starting at ₹18.50 lakh. This expansive 3,875 sq ft unit, complete with four parking spots, comes with a three-year lease.  The rental agreement includes a seven percent annual increase, culminating in a total lease value of ₹7.14 crore over the period.

news.title: Oberoi Realty infuses ₹268.50 crore in SPV for Bandra East railway land project
news.para: Centerstage Realty, incorporated on June 2, 2026, is a wholly-owned subsidiary of Oberoi Realty and will undertake the development of about 11 acres of railway land in Bandra East, Mumbai, leased by RLDA for 99 years.

news.title: Kochi metro phase II further delayed: Commissioning pushed to August 2027
news.para: Kochi Metro's Phase II project faces a significant delay. The ambitious plan to connect JLN Stadium to Infopark via Kakkanad will now be commissioned in August 2027. This postponement is attributed to external funding approvals and utility hurdles. Despite challenges, construction is progressing steadily, with foundation and viaduct works nearing completion.

news.title: Greater Bengaluru authority's five corporations collects ₹2,949.56 crore in property tax
news.para: Bengaluru East City Corporation leads property tax collection, achieving 47.35% of its target. Overall, the Greater Bengaluru Authority's five corporations collected `2,949.56 crore, 44.02% of their annual goal. Strong early payments, aided by a 5% rebate, contributed to improved revenue, though North City Corporation saw a decline.

news.title: Vijayawada civic body reviews progress of QR-based digital door numbering project
news.para: Vijayawada Municipal Corporation reviewed its QR code-based digital door numbering project, aiming to assign unique digital identities to properties for enhanced civic services. Officials were directed to ensure accuracy in data collection, mapping, and integration with the ERP system to boost transparency and efficiency.

news.title: KIADB to digitise land management process in 100 days, says industries' minister
news.para: Karnataka's Industries Minister M B Patil announced a 100-day deadline for digitizing the entire land management and plot allotment process of the KIADB. This initiative aims to boost transparency, streamline investor services, and enhance record management by creating an Integrated Land Management System for all industrial plots across the state.

news.title: Pimpri Chinchwad: PCMC property tax on common amenities draws flak from housing societies
news.para: Pimpri Chinchwad residents are protesting the civic body's decision to levy property tax on amenity spaces like clubhouses and swimming pools. Societies are receiving hefty bills, arguing this is a new financial burden on residents already covering maintenance and essential services. Officials claim it's an effort to tax previously unassessed properties under existing rules.

news.title: Gurugram: Man cons 242 home buyers through allotment fraud, held
news.para: Haryana Police's Economic Offences Wing apprehended a 33-year-old man at Delhi Airport, accused of defrauding individuals by promising flats in a Gurugram housing society. He allegedly collected crores from 242 applicants, failing to deliver homes or refunds. The accused, Anubhav Bhardwaj, was planning to flee the country when arrested.

news.title: Punjab government to formulate policy to provide relief to residents of unauthorised colonies: CM
news.para: The Punjab government is creating a new policy to help citizens who invested in unauthorized colonies. Many families lost their savings due to dishonest developers. These developers sold plots without proper approvals, leaving residents without basic facilities like electricity and water. The government will take strict action against these developers.

news.title: Reliance Industries-led consortium wins bid for 101-acre Mumbai slum redevelopment project
news.para: A Reliance-led consortium has secured the bid for the massive Juhu Galli slum redevelopment in Mumbai. This project will build over 28,000 homes for residents. The Maharashtra government's cluster redevelopment policy guides this initiative. The developer must provide significant financial support for resident transit accommodation. This marks a major step in large-scale slum redevelopment in the city.

news.title: NGT asks Uttar Pradesh to probe illegal groundwater extraction by Noida realty firms
news.para: The National Green Tribunal has ordered the state groundwater department to investigate illegal groundwater extraction by real estate companies in Noida and Greater Noida. This action follows a petition alleging widespread dewatering by builders for high-rise construction. The department must complete its investigation and take remedial and punitive action within three months.

news.title: ED searches continue at Jalandhar businessman’s premises
news.para: Enforcement Directorate searches at Jalandhar businessman Amit Bajaj's properties continued for a second day as part of a money laundering probe linked to former minister Sanjeev Arora. The ED conducted searches at six locations across multiple states. The raids have sparked political controversy, with AAP accusing central agencies of targeting traders and BJP criticizing AAP for communalizing the issue.

news.title: Maharashtra to introduce separate ready reckoner rates for towers, slums after micro-zoning survey
news.para: Maharashtra will introduce separate Ready Reckoner (RR) rates for high-rises and slum clusters within the same locality following a statewide micro-zoning exercise. This move aims to make property valuations more realistic and equitable, providing relief to middle-class homebuyers and slumdwellers. The revised RR framework is expected to be implemented from the next financial year.

news.title: Delhi: Regional Plan 2041 envisions fast trains, new townships
news.para: The Regional Plan 2041 proposes developing eight new greenfield townships and a high-speed transport network to enable 30-minute travel between Delhi and major NCR cities. This strategy aims for decentralized urban growth with self-sustaining settlements and modern infrastructure, addressing the projected accommodation needs of over 3 crore additional residents by 2030.

news.title: RBI issues final norms on easing bank lending to REITs, InvITs with prudential safeguards
news.para: The Reserve Bank of India has issued final amendments allowing commercial banks to lend to Real Estate Investment Trusts (REITs) and Infrastructure Investment Trusts (InvITs). These directions, incorporating stakeholder feedback, introduce prudential safeguards on exposure limits, asset quality, and repayment structures, while rejecting financing for land acquisition and under-construction assets.

news.title: DDA auction of 142 properties generates ₹1,300 crore revenue
news.para: The Delhi Development Authority (DDA) has successfully auctioned 142 properties, generating Rs 1,321 crore in revenue. This mega e-auction saw significant investor participation, realizing nearly three times the cumulative reserve price. The initiative aligns with the Lieutenant Governor's vision for developing Delhi as a global city with sustainable investment and world-class housing.

news.title: Cabinet approves phase 2(A) of Ahmedabad metro rail project
news.para: The Union Cabinet has approved Phase 2(A) of the Ahmedabad Metro Rail project, a 6.032-km extension costing Rs 2,169.04 crore. This expansion will integrate with the existing corridor, adding four elevated and one underground station to enhance connectivity and stimulate economic activity in the city.

news.title: Ranchi civic body intensifies crackdown on illegal buildings over water bodies
news.para: Ranchi Municipal Corporation is intensifying its crackdown on encroachments affecting water bodies and drainage channels. Recent demolitions at Bandhgari and Kanke dam removed illegal structures and piggeries. A comprehensive action plan and special task force are being formed to protect Kanke dam, with surveys also underway along the Harmu river corridor.
